# Official WCAG sources

## Pinned verification

Last verified from official W3C pages: **2026-07-17**.

At that date:

- The latest stable WCAG Recommendation suitable for an A/AA conformance audit is **Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2**.
- The canonical latest-published URL is <https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/>.
- The current Recommendation is dated **12 December 2024** and incorporates errata into the original 5 October 2023 Recommendation.
- W3C recommends using WCAG 2.2. Content conforming to WCAG 2.2 also conforms to WCAG 2.1 and 2.0, except that organizations separately required to report against earlier versions may still need to account for obsolete criterion 4.1.1 Parsing.
- WCAG 3 remains an early draft and is not the basis for this Level AA audit workflow.

Do not treat this snapshot as permanently current. Refresh it before each audit when network access is available.

## Normative source

- WCAG 2.2 Recommendation: <https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/>
- Dated 12 December 2024 Recommendation: <https://www.w3.org/TR/2024/REC-WCAG22-20241212/>
- WCAG 2.2 errata: <https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/errata/>
- Publication history: <https://www.w3.org/standards/history/WCAG22/>

The success criteria, definitions, and conformance requirements in the Recommendation are normative unless marked otherwise.

## Informative supporting sources

- WCAG overview and version status: <https://www.w3.org/WAI/standards-guidelines/wcag/>
- How to Meet WCAG 2.2 Quick Reference: <https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/quickref/>
- Understanding WCAG 2.2: <https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/>
- Techniques for WCAG 2.2: <https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Techniques/>
- ACT Rules: <https://www.w3.org/WAI/standards-guidelines/act/rules/>

These resources aid interpretation and testing but do not create additional conformance requirements.

## Refresh protocol

1. Fetch the WCAG overview and canonical WCAG Recommendation from W3C.
2. Verify the latest stable WCAG 2.x Recommendation, publication date, status, and errata link.
3. Check whether W3C now recommends a newer stable version for conformance.
4. Record the check date and exact canonical URL in the audit report.
5. If sources disagree, use the W3C Recommendation status and publication history, disclose the discrepancy, and do not guess.
6. Never replace the stable conformance target with an editor's draft or WCAG 3 draft unless the user explicitly requests a separate exploratory review.
